notes: SQLite uses a dynamic type system with type affinity. The declared type name influences affinity rules but any column can store any value. SQLite recognizes 5 storage classes: NULL, INTEGER, REAL, TEXT, BLOB.

types.name: INTEGER
types.description: Whole number. SQLite stores in 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, or 8 bytes depending on the value magnitude. INTEGER PRIMARY KEY is the rowid alias (auto-increment).

types.name: REAL
types.description: Floating-point number stored as an 8-byte IEEE 754 double.

types.name: TEXT
types.description: String encoded in UTF-8, UTF-16BE, or UTF-16LE.

types.name: VARCHAR
types.description: Maps to TEXT affinity. Length n is ignored at the storage level but is preserved in the schema.
types.notes: Length limit is not enforced by SQLite — any length of text is accepted.

types.name: BLOB
types.description: Binary data stored exactly as input. No type affinity conversions applied.

types.name: NUMERIC
types.description: Converts text/blob to integer or real if lossless. Falls back to text otherwise.
types.notes: SQLite does not enforce decimal precision; values are stored as INTEGER or REAL.

types.name: BOOLEAN
types.description: No native boolean type. Convention: 0 = FALSE, 1 = TRUE stored as INTEGER.

types.name: DATE
types.description: No native date type. Store as TEXT (ISO 8601: 'YYYY-MM-DD'), REAL (Julian day), or INTEGER (Unix timestamp).
types.notes: SQLite date functions work with all three representations.

types.name: DATETIME
types.description: No native datetime type. Store as TEXT ('YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'), REAL (Julian day), or INTEGER (Unix epoch).
